name: Transnevada Route J
name_original: Tajo del Cortés — circular desde Pitres por el mirador de los Tajos de Ángel
description: Transnevada Route J is a moderate 12.1-mile mountain biking trail in Sierra Nevada National Park, featuring a gorge and a viewpoint.
region_intro: Hiking around La Taha, located in the Alpujarras region of Granada, Spain, offers diverse landscapes on the south-facing slopes overlooking the Río Trevélez. The area is characterized by its position within the Sierra Nevada Mountains, providing access to high-mountain trails and views of the Poqueira ravine. Hikers can explore ancient whitewashed villages, lush valleys, and varied topography. Best hiking trails around La Taha The most popular hiking route is La Tahá Circular Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This route connects several ancient villages, offering insights into the local culture and landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pitres - Acequia Gorda - Pórtugos Trail, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. This trail winds through the scenic Río Trevélez valley, connecting charming villages. Local hikers also love the Path from Pitres to Mecina – Ferreirola loop from La Taha, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through traditional Alpujarran villages, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around La Taha is defined by high-mountain trails in the Sierra Nevada, the scenic Río Trevélez valley, and ancient whitewashed villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate village loops to more challenging mountain paths. The routes in La Taha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore La Taha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Taha?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails in La Taha, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging mountain paths. Specifically, you'll find 12 easy routes, 25 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes to explore.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Taha?
A: The trails in La Taha are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the charm of the ancient villages, and the stunning views of the Sierra Nevada mountains and the Poqueira ravine.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Taha?
A: Yes, La Taha offers several excellent circular routes that connect the picturesque villages. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980683108/la-taha-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tahá Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to experience the local culture and landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21836623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path from Pitres to Mecina – Ferreirola loop from La Taha&lt;/a&gt;, leading through traditional Alpujarran villages.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in La Taha?
A: Yes, the region features natural attractions like waterfalls. The Tajo de Cortés Waterfall, located near Pitres and Pórtugos, is an impressive natural feature accessible via hiking routes. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2777071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lanjarón River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; in the broader area.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in La Taha?
A: La Taha offers a range of trails, including easier options suitable for families. While specific 'family-friendly' designations vary, the numerous village-connecting paths and shorter circular routes provide gentler terrain and cultural interest for all ages. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' in the komoot guide.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while hiking?
A: Hiking in La Taha offers diverse landscapes. You'll traverse the south-facing slopes of the Sierra Nevada Mountains, providing access to high-mountain trails and magnificent views. The trails also wind through the scenic Río Trevélez valley, lush valleys, and canyons, often with views of the Poqueira ravine and its famous white villages.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the trails?
A: Absolutely. La Taha is rich in stunning vistas and historic sites. Key viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/310985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views of the Poqueira ravine and villages like Bubión and Capileira. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31567117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint – View of Bubión and Capileira loop from La Taha&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that takes you to this spot. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Veleta viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; in the wider region.

Q: Which villages can I explore on foot in La Taha?
A: La Taha is comprised of several picturesque ancient villages that are perfect for exploring on foot. These include Pitres (the administrative center), Mecina Fondales (Mecina, Mecinilla, and Fondales), and Ferreirola, with its smaller settlement of Atalbéitar. Many trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980711586/pitres-acequia-gorda-portugos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pitres - Acequia Gorda - Pórtugos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, connect these charming locations.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in La Taha?
A: Parking is generally available in the villages of La Taha, such as Pitres, Mecina Fondales, and Ferreirola. These villages often serve as starting points for many hiking routes. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas upon arrival, especially during peak seasons.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Taha?
A: The best time for hiking in La Taha is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter can bring snow to higher mountain trails.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the villages within La Taha, such as Pitres, Mecina Fondales, and Ferreirola, offer various options for accommodation, including guesthouses and rural hotels. You'll also find local restaurants and cafes where you can enjoy traditional Alpujarran cuisine and refreshments after your hike.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in La Taha?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in La Taha, especially on public paths. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb other hikers or the environment.
tips: Spectacular 15-meter waterfall on the Bermejo river in the heart of the Alpujarra Granadina (Sierra Nevada).

If you want to access the viewpoint, you just have to walk along a paved road that takes you there in a gentle climb. The views from above are spectacular: you can see the high mountains of Sierra Nevada, as well as the entire Poqueira ravine and its three famous towns: Pampaneira, Bubión and Capileira.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike Transnevada Route J?
faqs.answer: Komoot estimates that mountain biking Transnevada Route J takes approximately 2 hours to complete. This duration can vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Transnevada Route J is rated as moderate for mountain biking. It features a mix of terrain with some sustained climbs and descents, making it suitable for riders with a good level of fitness and some experience on off-road trails.

faqs.question: Is Transnevada Route J suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, Transnevada Route J might be challenging for absolute beginners or very young children. Riders should have some mountain biking experience and a reasonable level of fitness. For families, consider shorter or flatter sections if available, or ensure everyone is comfortable with the distance and elevation gain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access Transnevada Route J?
faqs.answer: Specific trailhead parking information for Transnevada Route J is not available in our data. However, as part of the broader Transnevada network, access points often exist in or near towns in the Sierra Nevada region. We recommend checking local maps or community forums for the most convenient and designated parking areas closest to your intended starting point.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Transnevada Route J mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter notable natural features. Keep an eye out for the impressive Tajo de Cortés, a striking gorge, and enjoy panoramic views from the Tajos del Ángel Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Does Transnevada Route J intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares significant sections with other established trails. Approximately 75% of it follows the main Transnevada Ruta J. You'll also find it intersects with parts of the Transandalus (46%) and the Transnevada (Stage 6 / 8) (46%) routes, offering opportunities for longer rides or alternative loops.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike Transnevada Route J?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow and ice, making parts of the route impassable or more challenging.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on Transnevada Route J?
faqs.answer: As this route is located within the Sierra Nevada National Park, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles, including packing out all pet waste.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for Transnevada Route J, being in Sierra Nevada National Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking or mountain biking on established trails like Transnevada Route J within the Sierra Nevada National Park. However, visitors are expected to respect park regulations, which include staying on marked trails, not disturbing wildlife, and packing out all trash. For specific activities or larger groups, it's always wise to check the official park website for any updated regulations.

faqs.question: What kind of weather conditions should I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: Weather in the Sierra Nevada can be unpredictable and change rapidly, especially at higher elevations. Even on sunny days, temperatures can drop, and strong winds are possible. Always check the local forecast before you go and be prepared for varying conditions, including sun, wind, and potential rain or even snow outside of peak summer months.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like water sources or cafes along the route?
faqs.answer: Specific facilities like water sources or cafes directly on Transnevada Route J are not detailed in our data. As a mountain biking route, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration. Some villages in the broader Sierra Nevada region may offer amenities, but plan to be self-sufficient while on the trail.
